Streams live from Deriv's public WebSocket — pick any of the 10 Volatility indices above (no login needed).
A signal fires when price moves the same direction 5 ticks in a row. Synthetic indices are random — this marks momentum runs, it does not predict the next tick.
Charts moved to 📈 Indicator Charts — price line, 1-min candles, order flow, and all indicators are now on that page.
📐 Price-action overlays — computed independently per timeframe
ICT / Smart-Money structure: support/resistance · demand & supply zones · order blocks (OB+/OB−) · unmitigated fair-value gaps · liquidity at equal highs/lows · BOS / CHoCH · premium/discount equilibrium · OTE (0.62–0.79 entry) · trendlines (auto, from swing pivots) · AMD (accumulation → manipulation → distribution, ICT Power of 3).
🖐 Pan & zoom: drag any chart to move, scroll to zoom (Ctrl/Shift+scroll = vertical zoom), or use the ◀▶▲▼ ＋－⟳ buttons. Each chart shows the latest 50 candles and moves independently.
⚠ Order-flow note: synthetic indices have no real volume or order book. Delta, CVD, absorption, imbalance and the volume profile here are proxies derived from price action (the Live Tracker uses genuine tick-rule data instead). Treat them as estimates, not real institutional footprint.
Each chart shows the latest 50 candles for the selected market, pulled from Deriv and updated live. Drag/scroll to pan & zoom, or use the on-chart buttons. Switch markets from the selector up top.

Signal Log

No signals fired yet. The engine fires only when imbalance, acceleration and low exhaustion align.

How it fires — the EARLY BURST PHASE (upgraded): the engine reads each tick by the tick rule and fires at the birth of a move only when seven things agree: (1) TD% just broke the threshold, (2) acceleration just turned positive, (3) exhaustion is still low, (4) a first cluster of 2–6 ticks has formed, (5) trend alignment — the move agrees with the drift over the trend window (this is the big fix: it refuses to fire against the prevailing tape, which is what caused most reversal losses), (6) dual-window agreement — a shorter window confirms the imbalance isn't a one-off spike, and (7) expanding magnitude — ticks are growing, not dying. These extra filters fire less often but far more selectively.

Win / Loss: a WIN is a sustained 5-tick run in the fired direction — no meaningful reversal and net price progress the fired way. Flat ticks and sub-noise ticks (smaller than the noise-floor × average tick size) are tolerated, because a 0.00 or a −0.01 tick against a strong move is microstructure noise, not a real fall. Set the noise floor to 0 for the strict "5 strictly-consecutive" definition. The Edge vs Random tile compares the win rate to the 6.25% chance of four more like-ticks after a directional fire tick (0.5⁴).

🔌 Deriv connection not connected

Signs in with your Deriv account via OAuth2 (Authorization Code + PKCE) — the entire login, including the Deriv redirect and code exchange, runs on the mambafx-backend Worker. This page only checks /me/session, lists accounts via /me/accounts, and requests a one-time trading WebSocket via /ws/otp — it never sees an access token. Login state is kept in an HttpOnly session cookie, not in this page.
Account
—
Balance
—
Type
—
Real money risk. Connecting to a real account places real trades automatically once armed. Synthetic indices are random — no signal predicts the next tick. Use the demo account until you fully understand the behaviour, and only ever risk what you can afford to lose.

⚙️ Contract settings

Each trade is a Deriv Rise/Fall contract on the market selected up top (—). A “rise” signal buys CALL, a “fall” buys PUT.
Allowed Range: 1.00 → 5.00 · Step: 0.01
Shared across every bot's Martingale & Reverse Martingale progression (Trading Grid, Digits Bot, 5-Tick Engine, Only Ups/Downs Bot, ADX Bot, and both floating bots). All strategies still reset to base stake automatically after a win (Martingale) or a loss (Reverse Martingale).

🤖 Automatic execution — 5-consecutive-tick signal

When armed, the grid fires a trade automatically the moment a 5-in-a-row run forms on the Live Tracker (the same signal that fills the log): a run of 5 rises buys CALL, 5 falls buys PUT. Honors your stake, duration and max-open-trades limits.
Disarmed. Connect a token and arm to trade the 5-tick signal automatically.

📊 Signal log — 5-tick runs with indicator snapshot

Every tick, MACD(12,26,9), Choppiness(14), CCI(20) and Bollinger(20,2σ) are tracked in the background against the live price/time series. A row is written only when a run of 5 consecutive ticks forms. It shows the start time of the run, its direction, the price at which the run began and the 2nd–5th prices, then the indicator values observed at that run.
Time Dir MACDSignalHist Chop CCI BB %BBB upperBB midBB lower
No 5-in-a-row runs recorded yet. They appear here the moment one fires.
Reading it: the three columns show indicator values at B-3, B-2, B-1 — the three ticks immediately before a 5-tick run fired. MACD/Signal/Hist, Chop (≤38.2 trending), CCI, BB %B. In average modes each cell is the mean across all captured runs at that B-window position.
Trades are placed directly from your browser to Deriv using your own API token. Mamba FX never holds or transmits your credentials elsewhere. Synthetic indices are random-walk products — automation does not create an edge.
B-window — 3 ticks before the run fired (B-3, B-2, B-1)

Price path across the window

No 5-tick runs captured yet. Once a run fires on the Live Tracker, its indicator window appears here.
How to read it: the three columns are the B-window — B-3, B-2, B-1 (the three ticks immediately before the run fired). Each row is an indicator value. In average modes each cell is the mean across all captured runs, showing the typical indicator state before a run fires.
The analysis window is captured automatically for every run: 3 ticks before, the 5 run ticks, and the 4 ticks after. Averages are computed over all runs stored for the selected market. Synthetic indices are random — patterns here describe past runs, they do not predict the next one.

🔢 Last-digit tracker Green cursor = current last digit · updates every tick

Tracks the last digit (0–9) of every live tick price. The green-highlighted circle marks the current last digit. Frequency counts update in real time — useful for spotting digit distribution skew in the current session.
Frequency — last 500 ticks
Current Digit
—
Ticks Seen
0
Most frequent
—
Least frequent
—
Last 10 digits
—
Synthetic indices produce last digits that are statistically uniform over large samples — deviations from 10% per digit are expected noise, not a persistent pattern. This tracker shows the real-time distribution for awareness, not for prediction.

🎲 Digits prediction engine — —

Tracks the last digit (0–9) of every tick price in real time. Uses frequency deviation, gap analysis, a Markov transition matrix and a chi-square uniformity test to build a composite prediction score for each digit. Click any digit to place a manual Matches trade, or arm auto-execution to fire when the prediction engine's confidence exceeds your threshold.

Digit Distribution 0 ticks

Frequency of each digit over the last 1,000 ticks. Blue = highest frequency, red = lowest, amber = 2nd-lowest, green (with the pointer below it) = the digit that just occurred. The prediction engine's composite score runs continuously in the background — it drives auto-execution below but isn't shown here as a raw number. Click any digit to place a manual Matches trade.
χ² statistic
—
p-value
—
Entropy (bits)
—
Top digit
—
Longest gap
—

📋 Digit frequency table

Rolling window of the last 200 ticks. The χ² test checks whether the distribution deviates significantly from uniform (10% each).
DigitCountFreq %DevGap

🔗 Transition matrix (Markov)

Row = current last digit, column = next digit. Cells show the observed probability P(col | row) as a %. Brighter = more frequent transition. Useful for spotting digit pairs that recur more than expected.

⚡ Trade execution — Digits contracts

Places Deriv Digits contracts on the selected type. Uses the same Deriv connection from the Trading Grid page. Not connected — connect on the Trading Grid page first.

No trades yet.
The prediction engine uses purely mathematical/statistical methods (frequency analysis, chi-square uniformity test, Markov transition probabilities, gap scoring). Synthetic indices produce random last digits by design — deviations from uniformity are expected noise and do not guarantee future outcomes. Use a demo account until you understand the behaviour.

🩺 API diagnostics not connected

Runs the raw WebSocket calls directly on the connected OTP socket and shows Deriv's exact JSON response — use this to see proposal.validation_params (min/max stake & duration), contracts_for (which contract types/durations this symbol actually allows), and any error.details.field on a rejection. Requires Connect to be active on the Trading Grid page first — this page reads the same live socket, it doesn't open its own connection. 

    


    

      Diagnostics run against whatever account is currently connected on the Trading Grid page. Step 4 places a real contract at minimum stake (0.35) on that account — use a demo account while testing.
    

  


  
  

    

      
🧠 ML Feature Engineering — Derived Indicators

      

        Computes B-window derived features from the 3 ticks before each captured 5-tick run (B-3, B-2, B-1).
        Features cover: slope, acceleration, threshold triggers, interaction cross-terms, histogram momentum, price-EMA deviations, Bollinger squeeze, Choppiness regime, pattern alignment.
        Only B-window features are used — P1-P5 (run) and A1-A4 (after) data are not stored and cannot leak into the model.
        Ranking uses mutual information (MI), Random Forest importance (RFI), and SHAP value approximation — all computed on the training set only.
      

      

        
        
        Waiting for signal records…
      

    


    
    

      
📊 Top Features by Importance
        ranked by weighted composite score of MI + RFI + SHAP
      

      

        MI = mutual information with run direction (rise/fall).
        RFI = Random Forest feature importance (Gini impurity reduction).
        SHAP = mean absolute SHAP value (TreeExplainer approximation).
        Higher = more predictive for that feature at that window position.
      

      

        

          
            

              #
              Feature
              Window
              Family
              MI score
              RF imp.
              SHAP
              Composite
              Rise avg
              Fall avg
              Δ (rise−fall)
            

          
          
            
Click "Compute features & rank" after enough signal records accumulate (≥10 recommended).

          
        

      

    


    
    

      
🗂 Feature Family Breakdown

      

    


    
    

      
🔥 Top 20 Feature × Direction Deviation

      
Each bar shows how much that feature's mean differs between rise and fall runs — the wider the gap, the stronger the discriminating power.

      

    


    
All feature computations run locally in your browser on the captured signal records — no data leaves your device. Features with insufficient data (fewer than 3 valid values) are excluded from the ranking. More signal records = more reliable rankings. Synthetic indices are stationary random walks — treat any high-importance feature as session-specific noise until validated across ≥500 runs.

  


  
  


    
    

      
🤖 Prediction Bot — In-Browser ML Pipeline

      

        Trains three gradient-boosted classifiers (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost approximations) on the engineered features from your captured signal records.
        All training, evaluation, and prediction runs locally — no data leaves the browser.

          inactive — train models first
        
        
          
          
        
      

      

        When started, detects each B-window (3 consecutive ticks) in the live Deriv stream, predicts with all three models, then waits exactly 5 ticks to see if a clean run forms.       XGBoost, LightGBM and CatBoost are implemented as gradient-boosted CART-tree approximations in JavaScript. Performance metrics are computed on a held-out test split. Live Validation monitors the Deriv tick stream continuously — a prediction fires when 3 consecutive ticks form a B-window, then the next 5 ticks determine ground truth. Synthetic indices are random walks by construction — validate across ≥200 events before drawing conclusions.
    

  


  
  


    
    

      
⬆⬇ Only Ups / Only Downs Bot

      

        Trades RUNHIGH (Only Ups) and RUNLOW (Only Downs) contracts on the live Deriv WebSocket.
        Before placing any trade the bot checks a five-factor fingerprint (adapted from the attached Blockly strategy):
        MACD histogram flip · DI bias aligned · Bollinger edge · Choppiness trending down · PriceVelocity/ATR > 1.
        Only when all five agree does the bot fire. Uses Martingale progression (×2 stake on loss, reset on win).
        


        Six-price-point rule: a valid 5-run requires six price points:
        B1 → P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 each strictly higher (RUNHIGH) or lower (RUNLOW) than the previous. Any flat or reversal resets the count.
      

      
      

        not connected — connect on Trading Grid first

            Compares the exact proposal request this bot just sent against a SmartTrader proposal you capture yourself.       RUNHIGH (Only Ups) and RUNLOW (Only Downs) contracts require every single tick to move in the same direction. A 5-tick RUNHIGH needs six strictly-increasing price points B1→P1→P2→P3→P4→P5. The Martingale doubles stake after each loss and resets after each win. Use a demo account while testing — real accounts are subject to actual financial loss. All trades are placed through the connected Deriv account on the Trading Grid page.

5-min · 1-min · 10-tick candles, then DMI/ADX · MACD · CCI · Choppiness line charts, then continuous price line, and technical indicators summary at the bottom. Each line chart scrolls the last 200 ticks. 

      

        

          
          
        

      

    


    
    

      

        
🕯️ Live 5-min candles

        

          Waiting for first 5-min candle…
        

      

      
      
Each candle = 5 minutes of ticks aggregated in the browser. OHLC coloured green (bullish) / red (bearish). Wick = high/low. Volume bar height = tick count per candle.

Candles are built live from the tick stream. Tick Δ = up-ticks minus down-ticks (real tick-rule delta), tick volume = ticks per candle, volume profile = tick-weighted time-at-price (POC in amber). ⚠ Note: synthetic indices have no real order book — these are tick-rule proxies, not institutional footprint.

    


    
    

      

        
🕯️ Live 10-tick candles

        

          Waiting for ticks…
        

      

      
      
Each candle = exactly 10 consecutive ticks. Useful for very short-term momentum: fast moves appear as long-bodied candles; consolidation produces doji-like candles.

    


    
    

      
📐 DMI / ADX ■ +DI   ■ −DI   ■ ADX
        —
      

      
+DI rising and crossing above −DI signals upward momentum; ADX > 25 confirms trend strength.

      
      

    


    
    

      
📊 MACD (12,26,9) ■ MACD   ■ Signal   ▮ Hist
        —
      

      
MACD line crossing above signal line = bullish momentum. Histogram bars show the difference (MACD − Signal).

      
    


    
    

      
🎯 CCI (20) —

      
Commodity Channel Index. Above +100 = overbought (look for reversal). Below −100 = oversold. Between = neutral.

      
    


    
    

      
〰️ Choppiness Index (14) —

      
≥ 61.8 = choppy/range-bound (avoid trend trades). ≤ 38.2 = strongly trending. Dashed lines mark the two thresholds.

📐 ADX Bot — Only Ups on Rising ADX + Rising +DI

      

        Fires a RUNHIGH (Only Ups) contract automatically when both ADX and +DI have risen for at least N consecutive ticks.
        The bot reads the live indicator stream computed on every tick — no delay, no candle close needed.
        


        Entry rule: ADX[t-2] < ADX[t-1] < ADX[t] AND +DI[t-2] < +DI[t-1] < +DI[t] (3 consecutive rising values = 2 increasing steps).
        Both must be rising simultaneously. You can require more consecutive steps in the threshold below.
      

      

        not connected — connect on Trading Grid first

RUNHIGH (Only Ups) contracts require every tick during the contract duration to move strictly upward. The bot fires when both ADX and +DI have risen for the specified number of consecutive ticks on the live tick stream — not on candle closes. A minimum ADX value ensures the bot only fires in trending markets. Use a demo account while calibrating.

Score = 40% tick-count imbalance + 60% magnitude-weighted imbalance within the current 1-min candle. Positive = buy aggression, negative = sell aggression.

📋 Candle history — aggression per 1-min candle

    

      

        
TimeOpenCloseBuy TicksSell TicksScoreVerdict

        
No completed candles yet.

      

    

  


  

    Measured from the real tick-rule (up-tick vs down-tick), not simulated volume. A high score with low intensity is directional but quiet; a high score with high intensity is a genuine forceful push. Synthetic indices are random walks — this describes what already happened in the candle, it does not predict the next one.
  





  

    

      

        
📊 Rise/Fall Autobot

        
Scans every Volatility Index simultaneously, determines who controls each market, and trades Rise/Fall only at a significant zone with confirming rejection and candle-body signals. Uses the Trading Grid connection — connect there first.

        
        
Not scanning.


        
        

        
        

        
        

        
        

        
        

        
The bot can trade up to Max Concurrent Markets at once — each qualifying volatility index gets its own independent Martingale sequence, running simultaneously with the others. Duration is fixed at 1 tick; contract type is always Rise/Fall — neither is configurable. Each market's Martingale multiplier is shared with every other bot on this page — see the Martingale Multiplier control above. Tick-delay between trades is fixed at 1 tick, per spec, applied per-market. Target Profit is per-market: a market keeps trading — through as many win/reset Martingale cycles as it takes — until its own accumulated profit reaches this figure, then it's released and a fresh market fills the open slot. If a market hits Max Martingale Level, only that market is abandoned — the others keep running. Stop Loss is the one session-wide hard stop, checked against total profit across every active market.

Positive Rate per Lead Time

        
Negatives are a shared pool (they carry no Lead Time of their own — drawn from outside every positive window), used as one background-rate denominator for every row. This is the first real statistical signal: at which Lead Time does being close to Run Start actually lift the positive rate above background.

📊 Feature Distribution Explorer

    
Mean, median, std, min/max, and a histogram for every engineered feature — overall and split by class — plus Cohen's d as the statistical-separation figure. Sorted by |separation| descending, so the features that naturally distinguish successful market states surface first. Click a row to see its histogram. Read-only, before any model is trained.

🔗 Correlation Explorer

    
Pearson (linear), Spearman (monotonic), and binned Mutual Information between every pair of engineered features. High redundancy between two features means one may not add predictive value beyond the other — useful for guiding future feature engineering, not just model input selection.

🧪 Experiment Dataset Builder

    
Builds a Training/Validation/Test dataset from the permanent Market State Database according to one Research Configuration, and permanently records that configuration as an Experiment — the dataset itself is temporary and rebuildable on demand; the config is what makes it reproducible. Chronological split (earliest trains, latest tests) — never randomized, since that would leak future information into training.

—

—

    

  


  

    
Statistical Discovery Engine

    
Runs automatically on every experiment's dataset, before any training would proceed. Notable findings (Mutual Information clearing the threshold) are permanently recorded to KnowledgeBase.

    

      
Sample Count
—

      
Positive Rate
—

      
95% Confidence Interval
—

    

    
Training Gate
—

    
FeatureMutual InfoInformation GainStability (std)CompletenessMissing %VarianceMax |Corr|

Machine Learning

    
Trains the same XGBoost/LightGBM/CatBoost tree approximations used elsewhere in this app — not a second implementation — on this experiment's Training set. Best model chosen by Validation AUC (never Test, to avoid leaking test information into model selection); Test metrics below are the honest, held-out figure. Results are recorded permanently to KnowledgeBase.

    
    
    
ModelVal AUCVal F1Test AUCTest F1Test Accuracy

      
Not trained yet.

  


  

    
Automated Lead-Time Discovery

    
Replaces manually trying one Lead Time at a time: builds a separate experiment, runs discovery, and trains all three models for EACH Lead Time listed below, then ranks them by trained-model Test AUC (not statistical Mutual Information — that's a different, existing ranking) to identify the earliest reliably predictive horizon. Each Lead Time gets its own independently-reproducible Experiment record. This can take a while — it's training up to 3 models per Lead Time.

    
    
    

    
    
Lead TimeSamplesBest ModelTest AUCTest BrierOptimal?

      
Not run yet.

  


  

    
Experiment History

    
Every experiment ever run — permanent, append-only. Reproducibility no longer depends on re-entering the same config: each experiment freezes the exact snapshot IDs it used, so it can be rebuilt exactly even after MarketStates has grown. Click "Verify" to confirm.

    

      
ExperimentFeature Ver.Lead TimeSeedSplitTrain/Val/TestWinning ModelDateReproducibility

🧠 Knowledge Base

    
Every notable discovery ever made, permanent and append-only — "Experiment #18 discovers Lead Time 12, Mutual Information 0.18, Feature ADX" never disappears once found. Feature Ranking and Lead Time Ranking are derived fresh from this on every load, never stored separately.

    
    
  


  

    

      

        
Feature Ranking

        
Each feature's single best (highest MI) finding across every experiment.

        
FeatureBest MILead Time

          
No findings yet.

      

      

        
Lead Time Ranking

        
Each Lead Time's single best finding — which horizon has produced the strongest signal so far.

        
Lead TimeBest MIFeature

          
No findings yet.

      

    

    

      

        
Market State Ranking

        
MarketStates is immutable — clustering never writes a Cluster ID back into a stored snapshot; every stateId stays 'unknown' forever, exactly as recorded. This is computed fresh on demand, same as Feature/Lead Time Ranking. Plain k-means on ADX, RSI, MACD, ATR, +DI, -DI (standardized), seeded for reproducibility.

✅ Research Validation Suite

    
Everything here answers "should I trust this before training a model" — not model performance itself. Dataset Validation and Statistical Discovery diagnostics run automatically on every experiment (see the Experiment Runner page); the tools below are deliberately manual, since they're heavier and shouldn't run on every click.

  


  

    
Latest Dataset Validation

    
From the most recent experiment run on the Experiment Runner page.

    
CheckResultStatus

      
Run an experiment first.

  


  

    
Reproducibility Stress Test

    
Re-runs the latest experiment's exact config N times against the current database and confirms every run is byte-identical — dataset, discovery, and feature ranking alike.

    
    
  


  

    
Negative Dataset Audit

    
Negative sampling was wired in after positive sampling — this specifically checks for the ways a later, separate mechanism could go wrong: window violations, homogeneity, and class balance, against the CURRENT full database.

    
    
    

      

        
Positives
—

        
Negatives
—

        
Class Balance
—

        
Window Violations
—

      

      
FeatureNegative StdPositive StdDiverse Enough?

        

    

  


  

    
Knowledge Base Repeatability

    
Groups every finding by feature + Lead Time and checks whether the Mutual Information score has been consistent across separate experiments — only repeatable discoveries should influence future model design.

    
    
    
FeatureLead TimeOccurrencesMean MIStd MIRepeatable?

      
Not checked yet.

  


  

    
Benchmark Against Random Chance

    
For the latest experiment's features, shuffles the outcome labels 200 times (seeded, reproducible) and reports what fraction of random shuffles scored as high as the real signal. A low fraction means the signal is unlikely to be chance — a high one means the data needs improving before the model does.

    
    
    
FeatureReal MIShuffled Mean MIp-valueSignificant?

      
Not run yet.

  






  

    
🔬 Statistical Research Workbench

    
Discoveries are recorded permanently and append-only in KnowledgeBase (type: research_discovery) — maturity is never stored, only derived fresh from every piece of evidence a discoveryKey has accumulated, replayed chronologically. A Research Session runs automatically after every completed experiment. Five analyzers are currently registered: Phase 2 (Single Feature), Phase 3 (Threshold), Phase 4 (Feature Combination), and Phase 5 (Market-State Pattern) — see the three ranking tables below the Discovery Repository.

    
    
  


  

    
Scientific Assessment Engine

    
Reads ONLY the stored Knowledge Base — never fabricates, simulates, or infers unsupported discoveries — and assembles the full 13-section scientific report from what's already been independently computed and tested across every prior phase. Updates automatically after every completed experiment (click "Train Models" in the Experiment Runner); also refreshable on demand below. If the Knowledge Base has no real discoveries yet, this will say so plainly rather than showing anything fabricated.

    
    
Click Refresh to generate.

  


  

    
Research Configuration — Maturity Promotion Rules

    
How many INDEPENDENT experiments a discovery needs before advancing, AND (Phase 1) how consistent its evidence must be at that point via the Evidence Stability Score (0-1). Both must be strictly ascending. A discovery can never be promoted past Observation on the strength of a single experiment, or with evidence that disagrees with itself, regardless of these settings.

    

      

      

      

      

      

      

    

    
    
  


  

    
False Discovery Control (Phase 7A) — Whole-Repository Correction

    
Per-experiment Benjamini-Hochberg correction (Phase 0) already runs before any evidence is recorded at all. This is a SEPARATE, stricter correction applied across every discovery's latest p-value together, recomputed fresh on every load — Benjamini-Yekutieli, since it stays valid even though many discoveries here share overlapping underlying market data (BH's own guarantee assumes independence this platform's discoveries don't have). "Verified" maturity requires surviving this whole-repository correction, not just the local one.

    

  


  

    
Strict Out-of-Sample Validation & Calibration (Phase 7C/7D)

    
Three tiers, kept genuinely separate: Discovery (train+validation — where thresholds are found and tuned), Validation (the test split — confirms it wasn't overfit to Discovery), and Production Validation (the most recent 15% of ALL data for a market+feature version, permanently reserved and excluded from every experiment's pool before Discovery ever begins — not just a relabeled slice of the same data). A purge gap (Phase 7C) now also separates every split boundary by each snapshot's own recorded lookback window. Calibration (Phase 7D) asks a different question than significance: not "is this effect real" but "does the SPECIFIC claimed rate hold up" — evaluated exclusively on the Production Validation holdout. Enter a threshold condition (feature, threshold, direction — copy one from the Threshold Ranking table above) to test it against genuinely unseen data.

    

      

      

      

      

    

    
    
    
    

  


  

    
Discovery Repository

    
Every discovery ever recorded, derived fresh from its complete evidence history on every load. Stability (Phase 1) measures whether the evidence agrees across experiments; Decay (Phase 3) measures whether it's trending — a discovery can be perfectly stable (low variance) while still decaying (a steady, orderly decline), since these are different statistical questions. Significance (Phase 7A) distinguishes Nominal (raw p<0.05), FDR-adjusted (survives correction), and Verified (survives correction AND has actually reached Verified maturity). Replication (Phase 7B) counts genuinely non-overlapping dataset time windows, not raw experiment count — re-analyzing the same period repeatedly cannot inflate this. Prospective (Phase 7C) means at least one supporting experiment's underlying market data didn't exist yet when this discovery was first proposed — the strongest evidence against curve-fitting. Evidence Score (Phase 0) measures statistical strength alone; Scientific Confidence (Phase 7H) is a separate, complementary measure integrating False Discovery correction, Replication, Stability, Regime Validation, Transferability, Out-of-Sample Validation, Calibration, Contradiction, and Decay — a huge-effect, single-experiment finding can score high on one and low on the other. Hover a value for its detail breakdown.

    
Discovery KeyTypeMaturitySupporting ExperimentsEvidence CountStabilityDecaySignificanceReplicationProspectiveGovernanceEvidence ScoreScientific ConfidenceFirst Seen

      
No discoveries yet — waiting for the first completed experiment with enough samples to clear the readyForTraining gate.

    

  


  

    
Threshold Ranking — Strongest Discovered Cut Points

    
Best threshold per feature, ranked by Evidence Score (a weighted geometric mean of effect size, statistical confidence, repeatability, stability, sample size, and consistency across markets/lead times — hover a score to see its factor breakdown), not raw positive rate. Derived fresh from the Discovery Repository above, filtered to discoveryType "threshold".

    
FeatureThresholdSamplesRate (above/below)Effect SizeStabilityMaturityExperimentsEvidence Score

      
No threshold discoveries yet.

    

  


  

    
Combination Ranking — Strongest Feature Synergies

    
2, 3, and 4-feature combinations whose combined (AND) condition beats the BEST of every individual member's own rate, ranked by Evidence Score. A combination only appears here if it genuinely outperforms its strongest single component, not merely co-occurs with it — and every combo tested in one experiment is corrected together via Benjamini-Hochberg FDR before any of them are gated on significance.

    
Combined ConditionSizeSamplesCombo RateBest Single RateSynergyMaturityExperimentsEvidence Score

      
No combination discoveries yet.

    

  


  

    
Market-State Ranking — Strongest Repeatable Regimes

    
Clustered regions of ADX/RSI/MACD/ATR/+DI/-DI space whose membership genuinely precedes a run, ranked by Evidence Score. Each row's identity is a canonical qualitative fingerprint (which dimensions run meaningfully high or low), not an arbitrary cluster index — that's what lets the same real regime accumulate evidence across independently-clustered experiments. The Market State column is a heuristic, human-readable label layered on top of that fingerprint for interpretability — the fingerprint itself, not the label, is what the maturity engine tracks.

    
Market StateFeature SignatureSamplesRate (state/rest)LiftMaturityExperimentsEvidence Score

      
No market-state discoveries yet.

    

  


  

    
Regime Validation — Survives / Weakens / Disappears

    
Every discovery's evidence, grouped by the DOMINANT market regime each supporting experiment was collected under (reusing the same clustering/labeling as Market-State discovery, applied once per experiment to characterize its overall condition) — never pooled into one global number. "Survives" means the effect holds at ≥75% of its overall average with continued significance in that regime; "Weakens" means a real, same-direction effect remains but meaningfully smaller or less consistent; "Disappears" means no measurable effect, or never significant, in that regime.

    
DiscoveryTypeRegimeEvidence in RegimeMean EffectOverall EffectStatus

      
No regime-tagged evidence yet.

    

  


  

    
Cross-Market Transferability

    
Every discovery's evidence, grouped by which market (symbol) each supporting experiment was run against — reusing the exact same Survives/Weakens/Disappears classification as Regime Validation above, just grouped by market instead of market condition. Under the project's current single-market research scope this table will read "Insufficient Data" throughout, by design — it activates automatically, with no further changes, the moment experiments are run against additional Volatility indices. Transferability Score (Survives=1.0, Weakens=0.5, Disappears=0.0, averaged across judged markets) now feeds directly into Evidence Score's market-consistency factor.

    
DiscoveryTypeMarketEvidence on MarketMean EffectOverall EffectStatus

      
No cross-market evidence yet — single-market research in progress, as expected.

    

  


  

    
Automatic Contradiction Detection

    
Discoveries whose evidence REVERSES DIRECTION across distinct experiments — a qualitatively more severe failure than weakening (Regime Validation) or declining (Decay Engine), since it means the pooled claim may be actively wrong for part of the evidence. Contradicted discoveries are capped at Candidate maturity absolutely, regardless of experiment count. "Likely Cause" is a deterministic check — does grouping the conflicting evidence by regime or by market cleanly separate the two directions — never a speculative explanation.

    
DiscoveryTypeAbove-side ExperimentsBelow-side ExperimentsAffected MarketsAffected RegimesLikely Cause

      
No contradictions detected.

    

  


  

    
Research Reports — Automatic Notebook

    
One permanent, append-only report generated automatically after every completed research session — hypothesis, configuration, dataset, methods, every discovery touched with its evidence score/stability/regime/transferability/contradiction/decay status, maturity changes, conclusions, and future work. Every field is a direct read of what Phases 0-5 already computed — nothing here is a new statistic, only assembly. Click a row's Experiment ID to view the full report.

    
ExperimentDateDiscoveries (new/reinforced)PromotedDemotedDecayingContradictedConclusions

      
No research reports yet — run an experiment and click "Train Models" to generate the first one.

    

  


  

    
Data Provenance & Lineage (Phase 7E)

    
Every discovery's full chain: Discovery ID, Evidence IDs, each supporting Experiment's Dataset ID (reusing the existing datasetFingerprint), exact frozen raw snapshot IDs, Feature/Label/Statistical-Engine/Code versions, and the linked Research Report. "Verify Integrity" checks that every referenced raw snapshot still actually exists in MarketStates — operationalizing "every conclusion must remain reproducible from raw evidence" as a real, checkable test, not an aspiration. One honest, stated gap: raw tick data itself is never persisted (only derived, labeled snapshots are) — see the code's own comments for why.

    

      

      

      

    

    

  


  

    
Research Governance & Experiment Registry (Phase 7F)

    
Write down what you're testing BEFORE running the experiment — this is what makes the distinction between a confirmed hypothesis and a coincidental finding checkable rather than a matter of memory. Registering produces an ID: paste it into the Experiment Runner's Registration ID field before running. Leaving that field blank is honest too — the experiment is then structurally, automatically marked Exploratory, never silently treated as confirmatory.

    

      

      

      

      

      

      

      

      

    

    
    
    

    
Registration IDRegisteredObjectivesHypothesis FeaturesStopping Rule

      
No registrations yet.

    

  


  

    
Data Quality Validation (Phase 7G)

    
A mandatory quality gate now runs automatically inside the Experiment Runner, before any Experiment record is ever written — a dataset with a CRITICAL failure (duplicate ticks, clock discontinuities, likely mid-session interruptions, corrupted feature values, incomplete labels, data leakage) is refused, not just warned about. Gaps longer than an hour are treated as normal session boundaries (a warning, not a block) — see the code's own comments for why a large gap can't be distinguished from a dropped connection with certainty. Use this panel to check the raw MarketStates collection on its own, independent of running an experiment.

    
    

  








  

    
Njanja Analysis

    Institutional Market Commentary — regenerates automatically as the 5-minute candle updates.
